subject: Mozi Minute: Active Beats Passive
body:
Words I like: The market gives you 10% returns. Your business probably gives you more.

Mozi Money Minute: Active Beats Passive

People will spend 100 hours trying to make $100/mo passive when they could've made $10,000 getting paid for their active time.

Bad trade.

What everyone seems to forget is that the people with passive income often had massive active income first.

Here's some math that shifted my perspective:

Stock market historically returns ~10% annually.

Small businesses on the other hand can 10x in a year. That's 1000%.

Illustration:

Fishing company. $100k to invest. Two options:

- Pay taxes. Now invest $60k into the market and make $6k per year.
- Buy a boat: Pay no taxes. Boat makes $100k next year with 40% margins = $40k per year.

He buys the boat. Better decision.

Active income investing thinking process:

- Reinvest where you have control
- Stack active income first
- Think equipment/talent/marketing, not equities

Your business is the asset. Everything else is the hedge.

$100k to $1M happens all the time in business. In the market? That's a decade.

Talk soon,

- Alex
